impressum.title: Legal Notice (Impressum)
impressum.sub: Provider information pursuant to § 5 DDG · Think Future Technologies GmbH
impressum.body:
Provider Information

Company: Think Future Technologies GmbH
Legal Form: Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung (GmbH)
Registered Office: Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany
Managing Director(s): Vijay Gupta
Register Court: Amtsgericht Charlottenburg, Berlin
Commercial Register Number: HRB 253195 B

Contact

E-mail: info@tftus.com
Website: https://www.tftus.de

Tax Number

Tax Number (Steuernummer): 1137055850364

Data Protection Officer

A Data Protection Officer has been appointed pursuant to Art. 37 GDPR and § 38 BDSG:
Name: Vinod Sharma
E-mail: info@tftus.com
Postal: Data Protection Officer, Think Future Technologies GmbH, Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany

Online Dispute Resolution

The EU platform for online dispute resolution is available at: https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/. Think Future Technologies GmbH is not willing or obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board (Verbraucherschlichtungsstelle), unless otherwise required by applicable law.

Information pursuant to the Digital Services Act (DSA)

1. Central Contact Point (Art. 11 and 12 DSA)
Our central contact point for the authorities of the Member States, the European Commission, and the body referred to in Art. 61 DSA, as well as for users of our services, is: info@tftus.com

2. Notification and Redress Procedure (Art. 16 DSA)
In accordance with Art. 16 DSA, any person or organisation may report content on our website that they consider to be unlawful by contacting us at info@tftus.com. Reports should include: (a) a substantiated explanation of why the content is considered unlawful; (b) the exact electronic location (URL) of the content; (c) your name and e-mail address; and (d) a statement confirming the accuracy of the information provided in good faith. We will process all reports promptly, impartially, and objectively, and will inform the reporting party of our decision without undue delay.

3. Out-of-Court Dispute Resolution (Art. 21 DSA)
Users affected by decisions of our internal complaints management procedure pursuant to Art. 20 DSA have the right to refer their dispute to a certified out-of-court dispute resolution body. Details of certified bodies and applicable procedures are published by the competent supervisory authorities.

Disclaimer / Liability for Content and Links

The content of this website has been prepared with the utmost care and to the best of our knowledge. However, Think Future Technologies GmbH cannot guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the information provided. As a service provider, TFT is responsible for its own content on this website. However, TFT is not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances indicating unlawful activity. Obligations to remove or block the use of information in accordance with applicable law remain unaffected.

This website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content TFT has no influence. TFT assumes no liability for such external content. The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is solely responsible for their content. External links were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking; no unlawful content was identified. TFT shall remove any such links immediately upon becoming aware of any legal infringement.

Copyright

All content published on this website including texts, graphics, images, logos, and software is the property of Think Future Technologies GmbH or its licensors and is protected under German copyright law (Urheberrechtsgesetz – UrhG) and applicable EU regulations. Any reproduction, distribution, or use beyond the scope of copyright law requires the prior written consent of TFT or the respective rights holder. Unauthorised use may result in claims for damages and injunctive relief under German law.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.sub: How we collect, use, and protect your personal data · Think Future Technologies GmbH
privacy.body:
Think Future Technologies GmbH (“TFT”, “we”, “our”, or “us”) is committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, store, and safeguard your personal data when you visit our website https://www.tftus.de, use our client portals, engage our IT services, or otherwise interact with us. We are a technology solutions provider incorporated under German law and registered with the Amtsgericht Charlottenburg under HRB 253195 B, with our registered office at Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany.

We process personal data in full compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (“GDPR”), the Bundesdatenschutzgesetz (BDSG – Federal Data Protection Act), the Telekommunikation-Digitale-Dienste-Datenschutz-Gesetz (TDDDG), and all other applicable German and European data protection laws. This Policy applies to all individuals whose data we process, including clients, business partners, job applicants, and website visitors.

1. Scope and Applicability

This Privacy Policy applies to all personal data processed by Think Future Technologies GmbH through its website, client portals, mobile applications, communication channels, and in connection with the delivery of IT consulting, software development, and related services. It applies to clients, job applicants, vendors, website visitors, and any individual whose data we process in connection with our business activities in Germany and across the European Union. This Policy does not apply to third-party websites or services linked from our platform, which are governed by their own privacy policies.

2. Personal Data We Collect

We collect personal data directly from you when you fill out contact forms, subscribe to updates, submit job applications, or engage our services. This may include your full name, job title, company name, email address, phone number, professional background, curriculum vitae, and communication history. We also receive data automatically through your use of our website, such as your IP address, browser type, device information, operating system, and browsing behaviour (e.g., pages visited, time spent, referral source). These technical details are captured via cookies, server logs, and analytics tools to improve user experience and site performance.

In the course of delivering services such as software development, QA testing, AI solutions, or cloud consulting, we may process project-related data provided by clients, including business contact details or system logs. We do not collect special categories of personal data (e.g., racial or ethnic origin, religious beliefs, health information, or biometric data) unless strictly necessary for legal compliance, security verification, or with your explicit consent pursuant to Article 9 GDPR.

3. How We Use Your Personal Data

We collect and process your personal data only for lawful, specific, and clearly defined purposes. Your information is used to respond to your enquiries, provide and manage contracted services, maintain client relationships, send marketing communications (only with your prior consent), conduct recruitment activities, improve our website and service offerings, enhance security, perform analytics, and meet legal or regulatory requirements.

We process personal data only where a valid legal basis under the GDPR applies:

  • Article 6(1)(b) — Contractual Necessity: processing necessary to provide services, perform contracts, or take pre-contractual steps.
  • Article 6(1)(c) — Legal Obligation: processing necessary to comply with applicable legal, tax, accounting, employment, and regulatory obligations.
  • Article 6(1)(f) — Legitimate Interests: processing necessary for business operations, security, fraud prevention, analytics, and protection of legal rights, balanced against your rights and freedoms.
  • Article 6(1)(a) — Consent: processing based on your consent, which may be withdrawn at any time.
  • Article 6(1)(d) — Vital Interests: processing necessary to protect the vital interests of an individual in emergency situations.

You may withdraw your consent for marketing or other optional processing at any time by contacting us at info@tftus.com, without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to such withdrawal. A detailed record of all processing activities is maintained in our Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) pursuant to Article 30 GDPR, available upon supervisory authority request.

4. Cookies and Tracking Technologies

Our website uses cookies and similar technologies to provide essential site functions, enhance user experience, analyse traffic, and personalise content. In accordance with § 25 TDDDG (formerly TTDSG) and the EU ePrivacy Directive, we only set non-essential cookies with your prior, informed, and freely given consent, obtained through our Cookie Consent Banner.

You may accept, reject, or customize your cookie preferences at any time via the Cookie Settings panel accessible in the footer of our website. Refusing non-essential cookies does not restrict your access to the core features of our website. You may also manage cookies through your browser settings. We respect “Do Not Track” (DNT) browser signals where technically feasible. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data for cross-context behavioural advertising or any form of third-party data monetisation.

For detailed information about the cookies we use, see our Cookie Policy.

5. Data Sharing and Disclosure

We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. We share it only with trusted third parties who support our operations, and only to the extent strictly necessary, including: cloud infrastructure and hosting providers (e.g., AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud) operating within the EU/EEA or under appropriate transfer safeguards; analytics and performance tools (e.g., Google Analytics, configured with IP anonymisation and data minimisation); payment processors (e.g., Stripe, operating under their own GDPR-compliant frameworks and bound by an Article 28 DPA); professional advisors including legal counsel, auditors, tax consultants, and Wirtschaftsprüfer, bound by professional confidentiality obligations under German law; human resources and payroll service providers operating within the EEA under binding data processing agreements; and affiliated group entities under Standard Contractual Clauses and supplementary organisational measures (see Section 6 below).

Everyone we work with is bound by Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) pursuant to Article 28 GDPR, Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), or equivalent safeguards to ensure a level of protection equivalent to this Policy. A list of our current sub-processors is maintained, updated upon any changes, and is available upon request. We may disclose data when required by law, court order, or supervisory authority, or to protect our legal rights, users, or property. In the event of a merger, acquisition, or business transfer, your data may be transferred to the successor entity under the same privacy standards, with advance notice provided to you where legally permissible.

6. International Data Transfers

We may transfer and process personal data outside the European Economic Area (EEA). Accordingly, all transfers of personal data are carried out exclusively on the basis of Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) adopted by the European Commission pursuant to Article 46(2)(c) GDPR, in their most current form (EU Commission Implementing Decision of 4 June 2021).

Prior to any transfer, we conduct a Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) in accordance with EDPB Recommendations 01/2020 to evaluate: (i) whether the legal framework in India (including the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023) provides an essentially equivalent level of protection to that guaranteed within the EU; (ii) whether supplementary technical and organisational measures, such as encryption in transit via TLS 1.2+ and at rest via AES-256, pseudonymisation, role-based access controls, and contractual audit rights, are in place to address any identified protection gaps; (iii) whether the specific categories of data transferred are proportionate to the operational purpose and do not include special categories of personal data without additional safeguards under Article 9 GDPR; and (iv) whether data transferred to India is subject to retention schedules consistent with both GDPR and DPDP Act requirements, as set out in Section 7 of this Policy.

Our data flows with Think Future Technologies Pvt. Ltd. are governed by an intra-group data transfer and processing agreement incorporating the EU SCCs (Module 1: Controller to Controller, and Module 2: Controller to Processor, as applicable), supplemented by a binding and enforceable data protection addendum. This agreement grants TFT GmbH audit rights over the Indian entity’s data processing practices and requires mandatory incident notification within 24 hours of any suspected breach.

Where we use third-party service providers located in countries outside the EEA, including the United States, we ensure that all such transfers are governed by appropriate safeguards including SCCs, adequacy decisions where available, or Binding Corporate Rules (BCRs). We do not transfer personal data to recipients in countries identified as high-risk by the EDPB without implementing all required supplementary measures. You have the right to obtain a copy of the applicable transfer mechanism by contacting us at the details set out in Section 16 of this Policy.

7. Data Retention

We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected or as required under applicable German, EU, and international law, in accordance with the principle of storage limitation under Article 5(1)(e) GDPR. Retention periods are determined based on the nature of the data, the legal basis for processing, and applicable statutory obligations.

Upon expiry of the applicable retention period, personal data is securely deleted using industry-standard deletion methods, irreversibly anonymised, or archived in a restricted-access environment where further processing is not permitted. Retention schedules are reviewed annually by the Data Protection Officer and updated as necessary.

8. Your Data Protection Rights

Under the GDPR and applicable German data protection law (BDSG), you have comprehensive rights over your personal data. We are committed to honouring these rights promptly, transparently, and free of charge, unless a request is manifestly unfounded, excessive, or repetitive. These rights include:

  • Right to be Informed — clear, concise, and transparent information about how your personal data is processed.
  • Right of Access — confirmation of whether we process your personal data and a copy of that data with supplementary information.
  • Right to Rectification — correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data without undue delay.
  • Right to Erasure (“Right to be Forgotten”) — deletion of your personal data where no longer necessary, consent is withdrawn, or processing is unlawful.
  • Right to Restriction of Processing — temporary suspension or limitation of processing in specific circumstances.
  • Right to Data Portability — receiving your data in a structured, machine-readable format, where processing is based on consent or contract.
  • Right to Object — objecting at any time to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes.
  • Rights in Relation to Automated Decision-Making and Profiling — not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing that produces legal or similarly significant effects.
  • Right to Lodge a Complaint — with a supervisory authority, in particular the Berliner Beauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit (BlnBDI), Friedrichstraße 219, 10969 Berlin — www.datenschutz-berlin.de.

To exercise any of the above rights, please submit a verifiable written request to info@tftus.com, including your full name and sufficient information to identify you. We will verify your identity through reasonable and proportionate means and respond within thirty (30) calendar days, extendable by a further two (2) months for complex or numerous requests.

9. Right to Withdraw Consent

Where our processing is based on your consent pursuant to Article 6(1)(a) or Article 9(2)(a) GDPR, you have the right to withdraw that consent at any time without detriment, penalty, or adverse consequence. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to the withdrawal. You may withdraw consent via email to info@tftus.com, the Cookie Settings panel in our website footer, the “Unsubscribe” link in marketing emails, or by postal request to our registered office.

10. Children’s Privacy

We do not target or knowingly collect personal data from children. Our website, applications, and services are intended solely for professional and business use by individuals aged 18 and above. If you are a parent or legal guardian and believe that your child under 18 has provided us with personal data without your verifiable consent, please contact us immediately at info@tftus.com.

11. Data Security

In accordance with Article 32 GDPR, we implement appropriate technical and organisational measures (TOMs) proportionate to risk, including: encryption of personal data in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256); pseudonymisation and anonymisation where appropriate; role-based access controls and least-privilege access; multi-factor authentication for systems holding personal data; regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments; a dedicated Security Operations Centre monitoring systems 24/7; mandatory annual data protection training; and a tested Incident Response Plan.

Should a personal data breach occur, we will move quickly to contain it, investigate the root cause, and report to the Berliner Beauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit (BlnBDI) without undue delay and, where feasible, within seventy-two (72) hours, as required under Article 33 GDPR. Affected individuals will be notified directly where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms, in accordance with Article 34 GDPR.

12. Data Protection Officer

We have designated a Data Protection Officer (DPO) in accordance with Articles 37, 38, and 39 GDPR, who operates independently and is responsible for overseeing data protection compliance, maintaining the RoPA, conducting DPIAs, and serving as the primary contact point for the supervisory authority and data subjects.

Data Protection Officer: Vinod Sharma
Organisation: Think Future Technologies GmbH
Address: Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany
Email: info@tftus.com

13. Grievance Redressal

If you are dissatisfied with our handling of your personal data or believe your GDPR rights have been infringed, please contact our Data Protection Officer in the first instance (details above). Your grievance will be acknowledged within twenty-four (24) hours and resolved within thirty (30) calendar days. If you remain dissatisfied, you may escalate your complaint to the Berliner Beauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit (BlnBDI), Alt-Moabit 59–61, 10555 Berlin, Germany, or seek judicial remedy pursuant to Article 79 GDPR.

14. Updates to This Policy

We may revise this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in applicable laws, regulatory guidance, or our internal practices. Material changes affecting your rights will be communicated at least thirty (30) days in advance via email and a prominent website notice. Previous versions are archived and available upon request.

15. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

This Privacy Policy shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany. Any dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Berlin, Germany, unless mandatory provisions of applicable law provide otherwise, and without affecting your right to lodge a complaint with a competent supervisory authority pursuant to Article 77 GDPR.

16. Contact Us

Company: Think Future Technologies GmbH
Registered Office: Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany
Registration Number: HRB 253195 B, Amtsgericht Charlottenburg
Email: info@tftus.com
Data Protection Officer: Vinod Sharma
Website: https://www.tftus.de

terms.title: Website Terms & Conditions
terms.sub: Rules for using our website and services · Think Future Technologies GmbH
terms.body:
1. Introduction

Welcome to Think Future Technologies GmbH (“TFT,” “we,” “our,” or “us”). By accessing or using our website https://www.tftus.de (“Website”), you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms & Conditions (“Terms”). Please read them carefully before using our Website or any services offered herein.

TFT is a technology solutions company incorporated under German law and registered with the Amtsgericht Charlottenburg under HRB 253195 B, with its registered office at Immanuelkirchstraße 14 A, 10405 Berlin, Germany.

If you do not agree with these Terms, please discontinue using the Website immediately.

2. Scope and Applicability

These Terms govern your use of our Website, including all information, resources, and services available through it. They apply to all users accessing the Website from Germany, the European Union, the European Economic Area, and any other jurisdiction worldwide.

TFT reserves the right to modify or update these Terms at any time. Where changes are material, we will provide at least thirty (30) days’ prior notice via email or a prominent notice on our Website before the changes take effect. Continued use of the Website after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

3. Legal Compliance Statement

TFT is committed to operating in full compliance with all applicable laws and regulations, including:

  • General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR – EU) 2016/679
  • Bundesdatenschutzgesetz (BDSG – Federal Data Protection Act, Germany)
  • Telemediengesetz (TMG) / Digitale-Dienste-Gesetz (DDG) – German Digital Services Act

All personal information collected, processed, or stored through this Website is handled in accordance with these frameworks and applicable international privacy standards. Our detailed data processing practices are set out in our Privacy Policy, which forms an integral part of these Terms.

4. Provider Identification (Impressum)

In accordance with § 5 DDG (formerly TMG) and § 55 RStV, the following mandatory provider information applies:

Company: Think Future Technologies GmbH
Registered Office: Immanuelkirchstraße 14 A, 10405 Berlin, Germany
Registration: HRB 253195 B, Amtsgericht Charlottenburg
Email: info@tftus.com
Data Protection Officer: Vinod Sharma
Website: https://www.tftus.de

See also our full Legal Notice.

5. Intellectual Property Rights

All Website content — including text, software, graphics, design elements, logos, databases, and other materials — is the exclusive property of TFT or its licensors and is protected under German and EU copyright law (Urheberrechtsgesetz – UrhG) and applicable intellectual property regulations.

You may not reproduce, copy, distribute, modify, publicly display, or commercially exploit any content without the prior written consent of TFT. Any unauthorised use may result in claims for damages and injunctive relief under German law. Use of our trademarks, logos, or brand elements in any form without explicit written authorisation is strictly prohibited.

6. Permitted Use

You agree to use this Website solely for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms, applicable German law, and EU regulations. In particular, you shall not:

  • Attempt to gain unauthorised access to TFT’s systems, servers, databases, or networks;
  • Upload or transmit harmful code, malware, viruses, or automated data-mining or scraping tools;
  • Misuse any contact information or services offered through the Website;
  • Impersonate TFT staff, misrepresent your affiliation with TFT, or defame TFT or third parties;
  • Engage in any conduct that violates the Strafgesetzbuch (StGB), the UWG, or other applicable German or EU law;
  • Harvest or collect personal data of other users without their explicit consent.

Any misuse may result in immediate suspension of Website access and appropriate civil and/or criminal legal action under German law.

7. Data Collection and Privacy

TFT collects limited personal information to improve user experience, respond to enquiries, and provide our technology services. All data processing is conducted strictly in accordance with the GDPR and BDSG.

We process personal data only on the basis of a lawful legal ground under Article 6 GDPR (contractual necessity, legal obligation, legitimate interests, or consent). Data subjects may exercise their rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection by contacting info@tftus.com or by post to Data Protection Officer, Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany.

For full details of our data processing practices, retention periods, legal bases, and your rights, please refer to our Privacy Policy, which forms an integral part of these Terms.

8. Cookies and Tracking Technologies

Our Website uses cookies and similar technologies to provide essential site functions, analyse traffic, and enhance user experience. In accordance with § 25 TDDDG and the EU ePrivacy Directive, we only set non-essential cookies with your prior, informed, and freely given consent, obtained through our Cookie Consent Banner.

  • Essential / Technically Necessary Cookies: Required for website operation and security. Do not require consent under § 25(2) TDDDG.
  • Analytics Cookies: Used to understand visitor behaviour (e.g., Google Analytics with IP anonymisation). Only activated with your consent.
  • Marketing / Targeting Cookies: Used for personalised promotions or retargeting. Only activated with your explicit and separate consent.

You may accept, reject, or customise cookie preferences at any time via the Cookie Settings panel in the footer of our Website. Refusing non-essential cookies does not restrict access to core Website features. Our cookie list is updated quarterly and is available via our Cookie Policy.

9. Third-Party Links

Our Website may contain links to third-party websites, applications, or resources for user convenience. These external links are provided solely for informational purposes and do not signify any endorsement, partnership, or affiliation between TFT and such third parties.

TFT does not control or monitor the content, privacy policies, or practices of external sites and cannot be held responsible for any damages, losses, or obligations arising from users’ access to or reliance on third-party materials. In accordance with established German case law (including the BGH “Haftung für Links” decisions), TFT disclaims liability for external links where we have no actual knowledge of illegal content. Upon notification of any unlawful content on a linked site, TFT will remove the relevant link without delay.

10. Confidential Information

Users may gain access to confidential or proprietary information of TFT through the Website. Such information must not be disclosed, copied, or used except as expressly authorised. Any breach of confidentiality obligations may give rise to civil claims under German law, including injunctive relief and claims for damages under the Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG – Trade Secrets Act).

11. Disclaimer and Limitation of Liability

While TFT endeavours to ensure that all Website information is accurate and up to date, we make no warranties or representations as to its completeness, accuracy, or fitness for a particular purpose.

TFT’s liability is governed by German law. We exclude liability for indirect or consequential damages to the extent permitted by law; however, we do not exclude or limit liability for:

  • Damages arising from wilful misconduct (Vorsatz) or gross negligence (grobe Fahrlässigkeit);
  • Injury to life, body, or health;
  • Liability under the Produkthaftungsgesetz (ProdHaftG – Product Liability Act);
  • Any other mandatory liability under German law.

For simple negligence (einfache Fahrlässigkeit), our liability is limited to foreseeable damages typical for this type of contract, and only where a material contractual obligation (Kardinalpflicht) has been breached.

12. Indemnity

You agree to indemnify and hold harmless TFT, its directors, employees, and affiliates against any third-party claims, losses, damages, costs, or expenses resulting from your misuse of the Website, breach of these Terms, or violation of applicable laws, to the extent permitted under German law.

13. Security and Cross-Border Data Transfers

TFT implements appropriate technical and organisational measures (TOMs) in accordance with Article 32 GDPR and recognised international cybersecurity standards to protect personal data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure.

Where personal data is transferred outside the European Economic Area — in particular to our parent company Think Future Technologies Pvt. Ltd. in India — such transfers are conducted exclusively on the basis of Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) adopted by the European Commission pursuant to Article 46(2)(c) GDPR, supplemented by a Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) and appropriate technical safeguards (including TLS 1.2+ encryption in transit and AES-256 encryption at rest). Full details are set out in Section 6 of our Privacy Policy.

14. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Berlin, Germany, unless mandatory provisions of applicable law provide otherwise. If you are a consumer residing within the European Union, you may also benefit from any mandatory rights and protections available under the laws of your country of residence.

The European Commission provides an Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) platform for online consumer disputes, available at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr. Unless required by law, we are not obligated to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

15. Termination

TFT reserves the right to suspend or terminate access to the Website at its sole discretion for any violation of these Terms or applicable laws, without prejudice to any statutory rights of the user. Upon termination, provisions relating to intellectual property, confidentiality, limitation of liability, and dispute resolution shall continue in full force and effect.

16. Severability

If any provision of these Terms is found to be invalid, unlawful, or unenforceable under German or EU law, that provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable, or if not possible, severed from these Terms. The remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

17. Updates to These Terms

TFT may update these Terms from time to time to reflect changes in applicable law, regulatory guidance, or our services. Material updates will be communicated via email or a prominent notice on our Website at least thirty (30) days before the changes take effect. Continued use of the Website after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms. Previous versions are archived and available upon request.

18. Contact Information

Think Future Technologies GmbH
Christburger Str. 23, 10405 Berlin, Germany
Registration: HRB 253195 B, Amtsgericht Charlottenburg
General Enquiries: info@tftus.com
Data Protection Officer: Vinod Sharma
Website: https://www.tftus.de

cookie.title: Cookie Policy
cookie.sub: What cookies we use and how to manage them · Think Future Technologies GmbH
cookie.body:
This Cookie Policy explains how Think Future Technologies GmbH (“TFT”, “we”, “us”, or “our”) uses cookies and similar tracking technologies on our website https://www.tftus.de. It sets out what cookies are, which ones we use, why we use them, and how you can manage your preferences. This Policy should be read alongside our Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions.

We comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Bundesdatenschutzgesetz (BDSG), and the Telekommunikation-Digitale-Dienste-Datenschutz-Gesetz (TDDDG). Non-essential cookies are placed on your device only after you have given your free, specific, informed, and unambiguous consent through our Cookie Consent Banner.

1. What Are Cookies?

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They allow the site to recognise your device on return visits and remember your preferences or actions. Cookies cannot execute programmes or transmit viruses. We may also use similar technologies such as web beacons, local storage, session storage, and fingerprinting — all referred to collectively as “cookies” in this Policy.

2. Legal Basis for Cookie Use

  • Technically Necessary Cookies — No Consent Required: Cookies that are strictly essential for the technical delivery of the service you have requested may be placed without prior consent under § 25(2) TDDDG.
  • Non-Essential Cookies — Consent Required: All analytics, marketing, and functional cookies require your prior, freely given consent before activation. You may withdraw consent at any time (see Section 5).
  • Legitimate Interests: Security and fraud-prevention processing may rely on legitimate interests where such interests are not overridden by your fundamental rights.

Our Cookie Consent Banner is implemented in line with guidance from the German supervisory authorities and the European Data Protection Board. Pre-ticked boxes or inaction are not treated as consent.

3. Categories of Cookies We Use

3.1 Technically Necessary Cookies — These cookies are required for the Website to function and cannot be switched off. They handle session management, security, and accessibility. No consent is required.

3.2 Analytics and Performance Cookies — These cookies collect anonymised data on how visitors use the Website (pages visited, time on page, errors). Used solely to improve Website performance. Consent required.

3.3 Marketing and Targeting Cookies — Used to deliver relevant advertising and measure campaign effectiveness. May track activity across websites. Consent required.

3.4 Functional and Preference Cookies — Enable enhanced features such as remembering language, region, or display settings. Set by us or third-party providers. Consent required.

4. Managing and Withdrawing Your Cookie Preferences

You may accept, reject, or customise cookie categories at any time. Refusing non-essential cookies does not restrict access to our Website.

4.1 Cookie Consent Banner — On first visit you will be presented with our Cookie Consent Banner to accept all, reject all, or configure preferences by category.

4.2 Cookie Settings Panel — Update or withdraw preferences at any time via the “Cookie Settings” link in the Website footer. Changes take effect immediately.

4.3 Browser Settings — Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies via settings. Disabling essential cookies may affect Website functionality.

5. Consent Records and Retention

We record all cookie consents (timestamp, choices made, Policy version) for accountability purposes. Records are retained for three (3) years and available to regulators on request. Where consent expires or processing changes materially, we will seek fresh consent.

6. Third-Party Cookies and International Data Transfers

Some cookies are set by third-party providers who may process data outside the EEA, including in the United States. All such transfers are made under Standard Contractual Clauses adopted by the European Commission. Data Processing Agreements are in place with all third-party providers. We encourage you to review each provider’s privacy policy for further detail.

7. Do Not Track (DNT) Signals

Our Website respects DNT browser signals where technically feasible. A valid DNT signal will prevent non-essential cookies from activating unless you have separately provided consent. Not all third-party tools respond to DNT signals independently.

8. Updates to This Cookie Policy

This Policy is reviewed quarterly and whenever there is a material change in our practices or applicable law. The current version and date appear at the top of this document. For material changes, we will notify you via a Website notice or email at least fourteen (14) days before changes take effect.

9. Contact Us

For any questions about this Cookie Policy or to exercise your data protection rights, please contact us:
